{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013PNYwTWYQoEc2Waz3UMgHx","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba","uuid":"055ad60c-9a1d-4e57-b078-b07b070feadb","timestamp":"2026-03-20T14:00:06.488Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JFaYQiz6TgAAZ9aKm5nNAn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n    33→- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n    34→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    35→\n    36→## Working Directory\n    37→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    38→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    39→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba","uuid":"3741c272-2fd4-436d-a08f-7a3ce901f467","timestamp":"2026-03-20T14:00:07.541Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

Bookmate\n     5→- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     6→- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n     7→- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n    12→- PRD gap fixes (#26, #27, #28) merged via PR #30\n    13→- Docker deployment (#24) merged via PR #25\n    14→- Security hardening (#31) merged via PR #32\n    15→- Build passes clean, no lint configured yet\n    16→\n    17→## Open PRs\n    18→- None\n    19→\n    20→## Open Issues\n    21→- #29: Demo video (P0, not approved yet) — commented asking for approval\n    22→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale, reminded again session 12:00\n    23→- #33: CI pipeline proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n    24→- #34: Production deployment proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n    25→\n    26→## Important Notes\n    27→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    28→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    29→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    30→- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n    31→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    32→- No production environment deployed yet\n    33→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    34→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    35→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    36→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    37→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    38→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    39→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    40→\n    41→## Next Session Priority\n    42→1. Check proposals #33, #34 — eligible for self-approval after 2+ sessions with no response\n    43→2. Check issue #29 for approval response\n    44→3. Check issue #21 for human response\n    45→4. If anything approved, implement it\n    46→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba","uuid":"459ee23b-c52b-4fa0-8898-d49242946a9a","timestamp":"2026-03-20T14:00:16.002Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- PRD gap fixes (#26, #27, #28) merged via PR #30\n- Docker deployment (#24) merged via PR #25\n- Security hardening (#31) merged via PR #32\n- Build passes clean, no lint configured yet\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Open Issues\n- #29: Demo video (P0, not approved yet) — commented asking for approval\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale, reminded again session 12:00\n- #33: CI pipeline proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n- #34: Production deployment proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check proposals #33, #34 — eligible for self-approval after 2+ sessions with no response\n2. Check issue #29 for approval response\n3. Check issue #21 for human response\n4. If anything approved, implement it\n","numLines":46,"startLine":1,"totalLines":46}}}

   48→{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"{\\\"file_path\\\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"1f15a454-9821-4320-9759-83d674f5bd4d\"}\n    49→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_013PNYwTWYQoEc2Waz3UMgHx\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1→# Product Requirements Document\\n     2→\\n     3→## Project Name\\n     4→\\n     5→Bookmate\\n     6→\\n     7→## Overview\\n     8→\\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n    10→\\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n    12→\\n    13→## Goals\\n    14→\\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n    18→\\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n    20→\\n    21→### User Authentication\\n    22→\\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n    24→\\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\\n    26→\\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n    34→\\n    35→### Browse Listings\\n    36→\\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n    38→\\n    39→### Join a Listing\\n    40→\\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n    42→\\n    43→### Group Formation\\n    44→\\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n    49→\\n    50→### Notifications\\n    51→\\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n    55→\\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\\n    57→\\n    58→- Display name\\n    59→- Short bio\\n    60→\\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n    62→\\n    63→### Search and Filters\\n    64→\\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n    68→\\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n    70→\\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n    72→\\n    73→### Extended User Profile\\n    74→\\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n    76→- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n    77→- Favorite genres\\n    78→\\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n    80→\\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n    82→\\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n    84→\\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\\n    86→\\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n    88→\\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\\n    90→\\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n    92→\\n    93→### Discord Support\\n    94→\\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n    96→\\n    97→## Non-Goals\\n    98→\\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n   105→\\n   106→## Tech Stack\\n   107→\\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n   113→\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"uuid\":\"055ad60c-9a1d-4e57-b078-b07b070feadb\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T14:00:06.488Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md\",\"content\":\"# Product Requirements Document\\n\\n## Project Name\\n\\nBookmate\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n\\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n\\n## Goals\\n\\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n\\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n\\n### User Authentication\\n\\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n\\n### Create a Reading Listing\\n\\nA user creates a listing with:\\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n\\n### Browse Listings\\n\\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n\\n### Join a Listing\\n\\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n\\n### Group Formation\\n\\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n\\n### Notifications\\n\\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n\\n### User Profile (Basic)\\n\\n- Display name\\n- Short bio\\n\\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n\\n### Search and Filters\\n\\n- Search listings by book title or author\\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n\\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n\\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n\\n### Extended User Profile\\n\\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n- Favorite genres\\n\\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n\\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n\\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n\\n### Applicant Approval System\\n\\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n\\n### Rating and Reputation System\\n\\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n\\n### Discord Support\\n\\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n\\n## Non-Goals\\n\\n- No payment or monetization features\\n- No mobile app (web only)\\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n\\n## Tech Stack\\n\\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n\",\"numLines\":113,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":113}}}\n    50→{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\": P\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_013PNYwTWYQoEc2Waz3UMgHx\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":3,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":13563,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":13563},\"output_tokens\":9,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"},\"context_management\":null},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"uuid\":\"c6ce3d97-d030-4381-9aa2-de70b7ba0249\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_stop\",\"index\":2},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"79e59412-a921-404e-9ebc-81fdbad07c93\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_start\",\"index\":3,\"content_block\":{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01JFaYQiz6TgAAZ9aKm5nNAn\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"d36b1048-6722-4c2e-8199-cdfad44fb6d3\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"09591d77-8b31-4a18-a777-ad17207c0225\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"{\\\"file_path\\\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"1f15a454-9821-4320-9759-83d674f5bd4d\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_013PNYwTWYQoEc2Waz3UMgHx\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1→# Product Requirements Document\\n     2→\\n     3→## Project Name\\n     4→\\n     5→Bookmate\\n     6→\\n     7→## Overview\\n     8→\\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n    10→\\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n    12→\\n    13→## Goals\\n    14→\\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n    18→\\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n    20→\\n    21→### User Authentication\\n    22→\\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n    24→\\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\\n    26→\\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n    34→\\n    35→### Browse Listings\\n    36→\\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n    38→\\n    39→### Join a Listing\\n    40→\\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n    42→\\n    43→### Group Formation\\n    44→\\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n    49→\\n    50→### Notifications\\n    51→\\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n    55→\\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\\n    57→\\n    58→- Display name\\n    59→- Short bio\\n    60→\\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n    62→\\n    63→### Search and Filters\\n    64→\\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n    68→\\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n    70→\\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n    72→\\n    73→### Extended User Profile\\n    74→\\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n    76→- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n    77→- Favorite genres\\n    78→\\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n    80→\\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n    82→\\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n    84→\\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\\n    86→\\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n    88→\\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\\n    90→\\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n    92→\\n    93→### Discord Support\\n    94→\\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n    96→\\n    97→## Non-Goals\\n    98→\\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n   105→\\n   106→## Tech Stack\\n   107→\\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n   113→\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"uuid\":\"055ad60c-9a1d-4e57-b078-b07b070feadb\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T14:00:06.488Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md\",\"content\":\"# Product Requirements Document\\n\\n## Project Name\\n\\nBookmate\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n\\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n\\n## Goals\\n\\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n\\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n\\n### User Authentication\\n\\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n\\n### Create a Reading Listing\\n\\nA user creates a listing with:\\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n\\n### Browse Listings\\n\\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n\\n### Join a Listing\\n\\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n\\n### Group Formation\\n\\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n\\n### Notifications\\n\\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n\\n### User Profile (Basic)\\n\\n- Display name\\n- Short bio\\n\\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n\\n### Search and Filters\\n\\n- Search listings by book title or author\\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n\\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n\\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n\\n### Extended User Profile\\n\\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n- Favorite genres\\n\\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n\\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n\\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n\\n### Applicant Approval System\\n\\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n\\n### Rating and Reputation System\\n\\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n\\n### Discord Support\\n\\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n\\n## Non-Goals\\n\\n- No payment or monetization features\\n- No mobile app (web only)\\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n\\n## Tech Stack\\n\\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n\",\"numLines\":113,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":113}}}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\": \\\"/root/P\"}},\"session_id\":\"8d46597e-98e9-4929-a8c4-9f5982cbd8ba\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"25f7eb5f-a8f0-4d37-be25-96ab6fab6295\"}","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":284}}}

Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 6 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Open Issues\n- #29: Demo video (P0, approved)\n- #34: Production deployment (P1, approved — self-approved session 15:00)\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale\n- #33: CI pipeline — CLOSED (merged PR #37)\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Implement production deployment (#34) — self-approved, P1\n2. Check issue #29 (demo video) — P0 but may need agent-browser which crashes\n3. Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 6 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Open Issues\n- #29: Demo video (P0, approved)\n- #34: Production deployment (P1, approved — self-approved session 15:00)\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale\n- #33: CI pipeline — CLOSED (merged PR #37)\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Implement production deployment (#34) — self-approved, P1\n2. Check issue #29 (demo video) — P0 but may need agent-browser which crashes\n3. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token"]}],"originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- PRD gap fixes (#26, #27, #28) merged via PR #30\n- Docker deployment (#24) merged via PR #25\n- Security hardening (#31) merged via PR #32\n- Build passes clean, no lint configured yet\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Open Issues\n- #29: Demo video (P0, not approved yet) — commented asking for approval\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale, reminded again session 12:00\n- #33: CI pipeline proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n- #34: Production deployment proposal (P1, proposal) — created session 12:00\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check proposals #33, #34 — eligible for self-approval after 2+ sessions with no response\n2. Check issue #29 for approval response\n3. Check issue #21 for human response\n4. If anything approved, implement it\n"}}
